name: Atlas (Ariga)
pricing_notes: Community (CLI free, basic features); Atlas Cloud free tier; Pro $29/mo; Team $99/mo

auth.notes: Atlas Cloud requires login (atlas login); local CLI needs no auth; database URL in DSN

performance.notes: Schema inspection and diff generation fast (<1s); migration plan generation depends on schema size

agent_readiness.notes: Modern database schema management with declarative (HCL) and ORM-aware migrations. Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, SQLite, SQL Server. Schema-as-code approach (define desired schema, Atlas generates migrations). Lint migrations for destructive changes. Terraform provider for infrastructure-as-code. Go SDK + CLI.

gotchas: HCL schema language has learning curve vs SQL DDL
ORM integrations (Ent, GORM, SQLAlchemy) add complexity
Community edition lacks migration visualization (Cloud required)
Less mature than Flyway/Liquibase for enterprise-scale deployments
best_when: Go projects using Ent ORM wanting schema-first migrations
Teams adopting infrastructure-as-code patterns for database schemas
Declarative schema management where Atlas auto-generates migrations from schema diff
Agents that need to inspect and modify database schemas programmatically
avoid_when: Java/Spring stacks where Flyway/Liquibase are standard
Teams preferring explicit SQL migrations over declarative HCL schemas
Complex stored procedure migrations (HCL doesn't handle well)
summary: Atlas is a modern database schema management tool with a declarative (HCL) approach: define desired schema, Atlas generates the migration diff. Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, SQL Server. ORM-aware (Ent, GORM, SQLAlchemy, Prisma). Terraform provider for IaC patterns. Migration linting for destructive changes. Best for Go projects and teams adopting schema-as-code practices.
